regime: Broad Risk-On, Clean Trend Alignment (First Full Agreement in Weeks)
summary: Broad risk-on, and for once everything agrees. NQ closed +1.78%, ES +0.79%, gold +1.74%, crude +2.57%, and all four finished green and above their 1hr stops. The standout is MES: it flipped long and ended a three-session ES/NQ disagreement, the first clean index alignment in weeks. The tape was a gap up that held and extended, closing near the highs, the mirror image of Monday's round trip. Gold rejoined the move rather than trading as a haven, and crude kept its Hormuz bid. The one caution is correlation: with everything long into an earnings week (Alphabet, Tesla, Intel, IBM), a single risk-off headline would hit every position at once. Not financial advice.
psychology: The hardest trade today was the one that worked: buying the reclaim near 28,700 overnight while it still felt wrong, not chasing 29,363 once it felt obvious. Now that all four instruments agree and everything is green, the tape feels comfortable, and comfort is when traders over-size. Agreement lowers signal conflict, it does not lower risk; it raises correlation. Keep your size the same as you would on a messy day. The market does not pay more for conviction. It pays for position sizing that survives the gap you did not see coming.

event.name: No scheduled release (Tuesday July 21 carried no major US macro print)
event.surprise: None scheduled. Tape was earnings-positioning and risk-on.
event.nqReaction: MNQ gapped up, dipped to a 28,700.00 overnight low, then reversed and trended six-plus hours to a 29,363.50 RTH high, closing 29,290, up 1.78%. A gap that held and extended, the opposite of Monday's round trip.
event.crossAsset: Full risk-on. MES flipped long (+0.79%) and ended a three-session ES/NQ split. Gold rejoined the move (+1.74%), rallying with equities rather than as a haven. Crude extended its Monday flip (+2.57%) on continued Hormuz headlines. Every one of the four closed green and above its stop. Next scheduled catalyst is Friday's July flash PMIs; the week's swing factor is the Alphabet, Tesla, Intel and IBM earnings block. FOMC is next week, July 28 to 29.

observe.name: ES/NQ confirmation
observe.note: MES flipped long and now agrees with MNQ. First clean index agreement in weeks; the conflict flag that ran from Jul 17 clears.

risk.ddProximity: Not binding. Book entered flat, full daily budget intact.
risk.sizing: Full per the NORMAL band on intraday trend setups. MNQ ATR(14) fell to 93.68 from 132.28, so the volatility input is lower; do not over-size just because the stop sits far away. Lines that must hold for the long thesis: MNQ 29,079.83 / MES 7,509.15 / MGC 4,052.71 / MCL 82.78.
risk.openRisk: Correlated long stack. All four are long-signal and pointed the same way, so a broad risk-off headline hits every position at once. That is the cost of clean agreement: less signal conflict, more correlation.
risk.note: De-escalated to NORMAL from REDUCED. The ES/NQ conflict that held the throttle down cleared when MES flipped long. What replaces it is correlation risk, not conflict risk: everything is long together into an earnings week. No major US release until Friday's flash PMIs. The real hazard is the earnings block, with Alphabet, Tesla, Intel and IBM reporting. Earnings gaps do not respect stops, so cut overnight size and only hold positions you would carry through a gap. FOMC is next week, July 28 to 29.

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Strong map day. Three triggers, all three played out clean, zero failures. Rows 1 and 4 were the down-side scenarios and correctly did not trigger in a broad risk-on session.

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked
fullReport.html:
#Row (as published Jul 20)StatusGradeWhat happened
1MNQ closes below 28,762.41 on 2 hourly closesNOT TRIGGEREDn/aDipped to a 28,700.00 overnight low and printed one hourly close below at 28,717.25, but never got the second consecutive close the row required. Price reversed and ran to a 29,363.50 RTH high. The short scenario never confirmed.
2MNQ reclaims the 28,917.40 AVWAP on 2 hourly closesTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUTReclaimed off the overnight low and held above on consecutive closes. Every listed reference printed: the 28,966.97 NYVWAP, the 29,004.56 +1s and the 29,000 psych all cleared, and the 29,118.50 proof level fell too. Ran to 29,363.50 and closed 29,290.
3MES closes above 7,534.22 on 2 hourly closesTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUTClosed above on consecutive hourly bars into the afternoon and finished 7,542.75. The three-session ES/NQ disagreement ended, both index layers finally point up. The 7,545.48 +3s and 7,552.25 overnight high were tagged intraday (RTH high 7,554.25).
4MGC closes below 4,000 on 2 hourly closesNOT TRIGGEREDn/aThe downside condition never came close: session low 4,003.10, no close below 4,000. The two-sided bias named the alternate path and gold took it, resolving up through 4,023.90, 4,046.10 and 4,050 to close 4,081.60, +1.74%.
5MCL holds above the 80.49 stop on hourly closesTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUTHeld every hourly close well above the stop. The second long flip stood and crude extended to an 85.03 RTH high, clearing the 83.16 prior RTH high and the 83.35 +3s. Closed 84.56, +2.57%.

Score: 5 rows, 3 triggered, 3 played out, 0 failed, 0 mixed. Strong map day. Three triggers, all three played out clean, zero failures. Rows 1 and 4 were the down-side scenarios and correctly did not trigger in a broad risk-on session. MTD: 45 triggered / 25 played out / 5 failed.

COMPASS filter (Wed Jul 22): no top-tier scheduled US release inside the 13:30 to 13:44 UTC window, so the COMPASS session edges are eligible pending the live opening-range read. Regime is trend-up, which favors the AVWAP Hand-off and continuation reads over the range and back-into-range plays.

Calendar: no major US macro release Wednesday July 22. The week's swing factor is the mega-cap tech earnings block (Alphabet, Tesla, Intel, IBM). Next scheduled macro is Friday July 24 (July flash PMIs 09:45 ET, June New Home Sales 10:00 ET). FOMC is next week, July 28 to 29.
